Match.title: Match
Match.description: Represents a single soccer match and its associated data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Match entity.

startTime.description: Date and time when the match started.

endTime.description: Date and time when the match ended.

currentPeriod.description: The current period of the match (e.g., 'P1', 'HALF_TIME', 'P2', 'FULL_TIME').

elapsedTime.description: The elapsed time of the current period in seconds.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents an event that occurred during a match (e.g., goal, foul, card).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

eventType.description: Type of the event (e.g., 'goal', 'foul', 'yellow card', 'red card', 'note').

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the event occurred (seconds since match start).

team.description: The team associated with the event (e.g., 'home', 'away').

playerJersey.description: Jersey number of the player involved in the event.

description.description: A textual description of the event.

definition.description: Stores the current match state for a user. Data includes scores, timer information, and game events.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier of the application instance.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Collection to store admin users, identified by their unique user ID. The existence of a document signals admin role.

params.name: uid
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning:
This design prioritizes security and scalability for the 'Referee's Edge' application, focusing on simple security rules and efficient data access. It addresses the need for admin control over user access, score tracking, timer management, incident logging, and PDF report generation.

**Authorization Independence & DBAC:**
Admin user management is handled using the `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection. The existence of a document with the user's `uid` in this collection grants admin privileges. This approach avoids complex hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules.

**Path-Based Ownership for User-Specific Data:**
Match control data is stored under `/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/match_control/current`. This structure enforces clear ownership. Only the user with the corresponding `userId` can read and write match data.  The structure nests the match data which simplifies rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
List operations are secured by the path-based ownership model. Admins cannot list all matches, as they do not "own" the documents and would need to know or enumerate userIDs to read match documents.

**Invariants:**
The design ensures that the match data can only be modified by the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`). Timestamps and other data integrity checks can be enforced within security rules.

**Admin User Management:**
To support the user's request for admin control over app access, the structure includes a `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection. This collection stores user IDs of admin-approved users. The existence of a document in this collection grants admin privileges.

The application utilizes anonymous authentication in conjunction with custom tokens or email/password login for administrative approval purposes.

**Denormalization (Not strictly required based on current schema but good practice for future scalability):**
Currently, no parent data needs to be directly inherited for authorization. However, if future features require subcollections under `/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/match_control/current`, consider denormalizing authorization-related data from the parent document into the subcollection documents to maintain authorization independence.
